questions.q: In Bloom's taxonomy, which is the HIGHEST level of the cognitive domain (revised version)?
questions.explanation: The revised taxonomy orders: remember, understand, apply, analyze, evaluate, create — creating is highest.

questions.q: A teacher gives a short quiz BEFORE the lesson to find out what students already know. This is:
questions.explanation: Diagnostic assessment identifies prior knowledge before instruction.

questions.q: Which theorist is associated with the Zone of Proximal Development?
questions.explanation: Vygotsky proposed the ZPD — what a learner can do with guidance.

options: Adding a pleasant stimulus to strengthen behavior
questions.explanation: Positive reinforcement adds a desirable consequence to increase a behavior.

questions.q: Which is an example of a FORMATIVE assessment?
options: Exit ticket at the end of a lesson
questions.explanation: Formative assessment monitors learning during instruction, like exit tickets.

questions.q: Piaget's stage in which children develop abstract reasoning is the:
questions.explanation: Formal operational (about age 11+) involves abstract and hypothetical thinking.

questions.q: A lesson objective that begins with "identify" targets which cognitive level?
questions.explanation: "Identify" asks learners to recall or recognize — a lower-order objective.

questions.q: Bandura's social learning theory emphasizes learning through:
questions.explanation: Bandura showed people learn by observing models.

questions.q: Which classroom practice reflects learner-centered teaching?
options: Students discover rules through guided activities
questions.explanation: Learner-centered classrooms make students active constructors of knowledge.

questions.q: Intrinsic motivation is best illustrated by a student who studies because:
questions.explanation: Intrinsic motivation comes from interest in the activity itself.
